title: Constrictive Pericarditis
chief_complaint: My belly is getting bigger and my legs are swollen for 6 months. I can't breathe when I exert myself. I had radiation therapy for Hodgkin lymphoma 20 years ago.

physical_exam.Neck: JVD 12cm with prominent Y descent. Kussmaul sign (JVP rises with inspiration - PARADOXICAL).
physical_exam.Cardiac: Pericardial knock (early diastolic sound). No murmurs.
physical_exam.Abdomen: Ascites (shifting dullness, fluid wave). Hepatomegaly (pulsatile liver).
physical_exam.Extremities: Pitting edema to thighs. Muscle wasting.

hidden_data.echo: Thickened pericardium (8mm). Abrupt septal bounce (septal shudder). Respiratory variation in mitral inflow >25%. Dilated IVC with blunted collapse. Normal LV systolic function (EF 55%).
hidden_data.ct_chest: Densely calcified pericardium. No pleural effusion.
hidden_data.cath: Dip-and-plateau pattern (square root sign) in RV and LV diastolic pressures. Equalization of diastolic pressures (RA=RV=PCWP). RVEDP/LVEDP >0.8.
hidden_data.cmp: Albumin 2.5, Total Protein 5.5, normal LFTs, Creatinine 1.1
hidden_data.bnp: BNP 120 (mildly elevated - classic for constriction). In restrictive cardiomyopathy BNP would be much higher.

key_learning_points: Constrictive pericarditis = thickened, non-compliant pericardium restricts diastolic filling. Causes: Radiation, TB, post-cardiac surgery, idiopathic, uremia
Kussmaul sign = paradoxical rise in JVP with inspiration (normally JVP falls with inspiration). Classic for constrictive pericarditis. Also seen in RV infarction and restrictive cardiomyopathy
Pericardial knock = abrupt cessation of ventricular filling in early diastole. Heard as early diastolic sound (S3 equivalent)
Echo: Septal bounce, respiratory septal shift, thick pericardium. Cath: Dip-and-plateau, equalized diastolic pressures. Treat with pericardiectomy
